title: Elevated Histamine Model for In Vitro Study of Histamine Effect on Placenta
author: Obed Brew
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jigckbw

steps.title: Obtain ethics permission and patient consent prior to placental collection
steps.instruction: Obtain ethics permission and patient consent prior to placental collection.

steps.title: Record clinical characteristics of pregnancies for...
steps.instruction: Record clinical characteristics of pregnancies for placentae to be studied as Table 1 (sample Table 1 available from http://www.elsevier.com/__data/promis_misc/YPLAC%20Guide%20for%20authors%20Table%201.doc ).

steps.title: Collect from operating theatre term (38-39...
steps.instruction: Collect from operating theatre term (38-39 weeks of gestation) placenta delivered by caesarean section from normal pregnancy.

steps.title: Inspect the placenta and record evidence...
steps.instruction: Inspect the placenta and record evidence for gross abnormalities such as placental infarcts, excessive tears, necrosis, absent cotyledons, discolorations on the maternal and fetal surfaces, and anomalies in cord insertion.

steps.title: Cut 2 cm 3 of placental...
steps.instruction: Cut 2 cm 3 of placental samples randomly from at least 3 different healthy looking sampling sites about 5 cm away from the umbilical cord with sterile sharp scissors.

steps.title: Excise each sample to include an...
steps.instruction: Excise each sample to include an intact chorionic plate, intervillous space, basal plate and decidua.

steps.title: Wash each placental sample in phosphate-buffered...
steps.instruction: Wash each placental sample in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) solution (pH 7.4) containing 10% penicillin, streptomycin and L-glutamine (Sigma) to remove excess blood and blood clots.

steps.title: Transport each washed sample in 150...
steps.instruction: Transport each washed sample in 150 mL sterile pots containing warm PBS with 10% penicillin, streptomycin and L-glutamine immediately to the laboratory.

steps.title: Aseptically dissect micro explants (<50 mg...
steps.instruction: Aseptically dissect micro explants (<50 mg wet weight) of villous tissue with sterile scalpel from each sample.

steps.title: Incubate the micro explant within 30 min of delivery
steps.instruction: Incubate the micro explant within 30 min of delivery.

steps.title: Place each fragmented micro explant on...
steps.instruction: Place each fragmented micro explant on a mesh support in a 12-well culture plate containing 15mm diameter Netwell inserts with 74µm polyester mesh bottoms attached to polystyrene inserts (Corning, UK).

steps.title: Add basic media
steps.instruction: Add basic media: RPMI 1640 culture medium (Invitrogen, UK) supplemented with 2 mM L-glutamine, 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 IU/ml penicillin and 100 µg/ml streptomycin.

steps.title: Incubate micro explants in basic media...
steps.instruction: Incubate micro explants in basic media at the liquid-gas interface in 8% oxygen and 5% CO 2 , at 37°C for 5 days. Note: culture at the bottom of the well was associated with decreased explant viability and poor RNA quality.

steps.title: Replace culture media at days 2 and 4
steps.instruction: Replace culture media at days 2 and 4: place the media in a sterile container and expose to 8% oxygen tension for 2 hours before the medium change is done. Keep the time for the change of medium to the minimum (less than 5 minutes).

steps.title: To create the elevated histamine model,...
steps.instruction: To create the elevated histamine model, repeat media change on day 5 for elevated histamine treatment: Replace media for samples designated as dHDA samples with final media containing 100nM histamine in the presence of aminoguanidine (10 -4 M final concentration) Replace media for samples designated as nHDA with final basic media without aminoguanidine or histamine. Culture samples for further 24 hours

steps.title: At the end of the 6...
steps.instruction: At the end of the 6 days culture period collect the explants and store immediately in RNAlater (Ambion) at -80°C.

steps.title: Store pre-culture, 0 h control samples...
steps.instruction: Store pre-culture, 0 h control samples obtained from fresh placentae in RNAlater at -80°C within 30 minutes of delivery.
